commit.message:
Add component diagram in draw.io format

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix game hang after screensaver by delivering APU frame counter IRQs to CPU

The APU's isIrqPending() was never checked in the main loop, so games
relying on APU IRQs for timing (e.g. Double Dragon & Battletoads) would
spin indefinitely waiting for an interrupt that never fired.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add APU implementation for sound support

Implement NES Audio Processing Unit with pulse, triangle, and noise
channels, wired into the CPU bus and main emulation loop. Audio output
uses javax.sound.sampled at 44100 Hz.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix game freeze after title screen by adding NMI edge detection, PRG RAM, and DMA NMI handling

- Trigger NMI immediately when game enables NMI via PPUCTRL while VBlank is active
- Add 8KB PRG RAM at $6000-$7FFF so game state reads/writes work correctly
- Check for NMI during DMA transfer to avoid missing VBlank interrupts

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Implement Extended Link Opaque LSA parser and database detail display

- Parse Extended Link TLV (RFC 7684 §3) with Adj-SID (RFC 8665 §5)
  and LAN Adj-SID (RFC 8665 §6) Sub-TLVs
- Add show database detail output for Extended Link LSAs
- Clean up remaining debug println statements in ifsm.rs and inst.rs

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add create task dialog, auto-save, multi-file drop, and comments/data fixes

- Add CreateTaskDialog: when filters are active, Enter opens a dialog instead
  of inserting an inline block, supporting #hashtag and @mention parsing
- Add debounced auto-save (3s default) for inline note title edits
- Support multi-file drag & drop in BlockNoteEditor with parallel uploads,
  and inject delete buttons on file blocks
- Simplify comments UI: remove reply/resolve threading, flatten to a linear
  comment list with compact layout and toast on save
- Make comments fully optimistic: instant UI updates with pending-mutation
  guard to prevent realtime subscription from overwriting local state
- Fix foreign key violations: await note insert in Supabase before emitting
  label/assignee events in createNote and createNoteAfter
- Add optimistic assignee cache updates via event subscription
- Change default note sort from sort_order to created_at descending
- Fix GCal calendar fetch deduplication with one-shot ref
- Add is_default field to Contact type, fix Supabase type casts
- Remove note_comments updated_at DB trigger (managed in app code)
- Add i18n keys for create task dialog and comment saved toast (en/es/pt)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Implement OSPF SR TLV parsing and database detail display

- Move SidLabelTlv to packet-utils crate for sharing between isis-packet and ospf-packet
- Add OSPF-style SID/Label Sub-TLV parser (2+2 byte header vs IS-IS 1+1)
- Parse RouterInfo TLVs: SID/Label Range, Local Block, Router Capability, Algorithm
- Implement Extended Prefix Opaque LSA parser (RFC 7684) with Prefix SID Sub-TLV (RFC 8665)
- Add "show ip ospf database detail" output for Opaque Area LSAs with SR details
- Clean up debug println statements in nfsm.rs

Classify MethodInitialFinalSpecifiers → §8.20 [skip ci]

The test exercises the `dynamic_override_specifiers` — specifically the `:initial` and `:final` specifiers on a class method declaration. These specifiers are defined in §8.20 (Virtual methods), which introduces `initial_or_extends_specifier` and `final_specifier` as part of the `dynamic_override_specifiers` grammar production, and describes their semantics for controlling virtual method overriding.

commit.message:
fix: action item completion not refreshing tabs

The mutation was invalidating ['actions', userId] but the paginated
query uses ['actions', 'cursor', sessionKey, pageSize]. React Query's
prefix matching couldn't connect the two, so completed actions never
moved to the Completed tab. Fixed by invalidating with the base
['actions'] key which matches all action-related query variants.

commit.message:
fix: blob store JSON uses public access for reliable reads

Private blobs require signed URLs (getDownloadUrl) which return 403.
Switching setJSON to public access makes tokens readable via direct URL.
get() now tries direct URL first, signed URL as fallback.

This is the ROOT CAUSE of scanner 401s: tokens were written to blob
but could never be read back by cold Vercel instances.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
perf: zero-copy arrays, parallel bincount, skip extract_arrays

Three performance optimizations:

1. Zero-copy for read-only arrays: FE and cluster codes are borrowed
   directly from numpy via as_slice() instead of copying to Vec.
   Only y and X (mutated by demeaning) are copied.

2. Parallel bincount in demeaning: scatter-add uses chunked parallel
   reduction via rayon when N >= 200K and n_groups >= 32. Each thread
   accumulates into a local buffer, then results are merged.

3. rust_ols_from_arrays: new entry point that accepts individual column
   arrays directly, bypassing Python's extract_arrays/column_stack/
   astype overhead. The ols() function routes eligible calls through
   _ols_direct_rust which extracts Polars columns and passes them
   straight to Rust.

Benchmarks (2-way FE + 2-way cluster, N=1M):
  Before: ~203ms → After: ~165ms (19% faster)
  vs pure Python: ~980ms (6x speedup)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

HOTFIX: Fix 524 timeout — ThreadPool DB connection leak + notification caching

close_old_connections() only closes connections older than CONN_MAX_AGE (600s).
ThreadPool builder threads created new connections that were never released,
exhausting Railway PostgreSQL connection pool. Replaced with connection.close()
to explicitly release connections after each builder completes.

Also added 30-second cache to notification count API endpoint to reduce
polling DB pressure during high-load periods.

commit.message:
Add app theme system with Tufte-inspired Academia theme

Separate concept from color palettes: themes control the entire app
look (fonts, backgrounds, borders) while palettes control grid colors.
Switching theme auto-sets a matching default palette, but individual
goals can still override via the existing per-goal color picker.

- Academia theme: Palatino serif font, warm ivory #fffff8 background
- Theme picker in Settings > Display Settings

commit.message:
M4: Fix PHPUnit test discovery -- rename file to Rule_Engine_Test.php

PHPUnit 10 derives the expected class name from the filename.
Renamed tests/class-test-rule-engine.php to tests/Rule_Engine_Test.php
so PHPUnit can map filename -> class Rule_Engine_Test correctly.
Updated phpunit.xml.dist to use suffix="Test.php" directory discovery.
Added .phpunit.result.cache to .gitignore.

Result: 11 / 11 tests pass via vendor/bin/phpunit (PHPUnit 10.5.63).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add consistent hover and cursor states with design system tokens (#13)

- Add design tokens for interactive row hover/selected states and draft pill styling (light + dark modes)
- Add transition tokens for consistent animation timing and easing across the app
- Map new tokens in Tailwind @theme block for use in utility classes
- Add global cursor:pointer and transition rules to all interactive elements
- Update 14 components to use design-token-mapped hover classes (bg-row-hover, bg-bg-muted, text-text-primary)
- Fix selected state in MillerColumn: change from heavy blue (bg-accent) to subtle accent tint (bg-accent-subtle) with left accent border
- Fix draft pill readability: add dedicated draft tokens with proper contrast for dark mode
- Fix author empty string display: use || instead of ?? to show "—" placeholder
- Improve dark mode hover visibility: increase --color-row-hover opacity to 0.09
- Update design system documentation with Transitions and Hover & Active States sections

This provides a premium, cohesive hover/active state experience across the entire app, with proper light/dark mode contrast and smooth 100ms transitions.

commit.message:
Classify MethodFinalSpecifier → §8.20 [skip ci]

The :final specifier applied to methods is defined in §8.20 Virtual methods, which states the final specifier specifies that no further overrides of the method shall be allowed. No deeper-numbered subclause covers this feature.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: Buddhi substrate rewrite — MahaBuddhi + MahaCompression + IntentGuna

Replace hardcoded TaskIntent enum + regex classification with real
steward-protocol substrate primitives:

- MahaBuddhi.think() for cognitive frame (Lotus VM, zero LLM)
- MahaCompression.decode_samskara_intent() for guna classification
- SemanticActionType from substrate taxonomy (not custom enum)
- IntentGuna (SATTVA/RAJAS/TAMAS/SUDDHA) for tool routing

Pre-flight now deterministic from substrate cognition:
  approach affinity → function affinity → guna fallback
  → SemanticActionType → tool set + token budget

232 tests pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix network benchmark (pure Python, no curl), add stock boot + custom fan-out

Network benchmark:
- Rewrite all steps to use pure Python (urllib/socket) instead of curl
- Write scripts as files in sandbox instead of broken inline -c commands
- Add User-Agent header to fix Cloudflare 403 on download test
- All 5 steps now pass on all 4 providers

Docker benchmark:
- Add docker_stock_boot step: measures baseline boot time with stock image
- Now 6 steps: build, create (custom), verify, workload, stock boot, compare
- Comparison step now includes stock boot time in output

Fan-out benchmark:
- Add custom Docker image support for Daytona and Modal
- New fo_create_custom step: creates N sandboxes from custom images
- E2B/Blaxel correctly skip custom image step (no runtime build support)

fix: lint:ci の import ソートエラーを修正 (#342)

PR #341 で `crypto` → `node:crypto` に変更した際、
import ソート順が崩れて `biome ci` がエラーを報告していた。
`node:*` プロトコルは最上位にソートされるため順序を修正。

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: Initial release of changelog skill for Claude Code

A Claude Code skill that generates user-facing changelog entries from
git commits. Translates technical commit messages into plain-language
descriptions and outputs structured TypeScript data.

Features:
- Conventional commit parsing (feat, fix, perf, style, etc.)
- Semantic versioning with automatic version calculation
- Daily grouping (one release per day with cumulative versioning)
- Anti-duplication (consolidates related commits)
- INSERT/UPDATE mode detection
- New project scaffolding (creates types + data file)
- Preview mode and flexible time ranges

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
